commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=14becf63d3742c9dcebc547385aa8bf83f7dae65)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an account-scoped Telegram active-run ingress key for text follow-ups, documents the behavior, exports reply-fence lane activity, and adds a runtime fallback/test for legacy bare root-key file SecretRefs.

PR surface: Source +115, Tests +118, Docs 0. Total +233 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level. Current main serializes same-topic Telegram text under one grammY sequentialize key and readJsonPointer rejects non-slash file ids; I did not run a live Telegram reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the contributor must supply real Telegram proof and maintainers should accept the SecretRef runtime-compatibility exception; no ClawSweeper repair marker should run while proof is insufficient.

target.title: [codex] Fix Telegram active-run ingress and legacy file SecretRefs
target.body:
## Summary
- route plain-text Telegram follow-ups in an active reply fence through a narrow ingress lane so they can reach queue/steer handling without waiting behind the running handler
- keep media/media-group Telegram updates on the normal serialized lane for compatibility
- resolve legacy bare root-key file SecretRefs when the JSON payload has that exact root property, while preserving JSON-pointer schema/parity checks

## Root Cause
Telegram used one per-chat/per-thread sequential lane for all inbound updates, so follow-up text sent during an active run waited for the current handler to finish before it could affect the run. Separately, older managed secrets configs can contain file SecretRef ids that name root JSON keys directly; newer runtime resolution required JSON pointers and could fail gateway startup before users had a chance to migrate.

## Validation
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts src/secrets/resolve.test.ts src/secrets/exec-secret-ref-id-parity.test.ts src/config/config.secrets-schema.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/telegram/src/bot-core.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/telegram-reply-fence.ts src/secrets/resolve.ts src/secrets/resolve.test.ts`

## Notes
- Latest upstream already treats ChatGPT/Codex auth as the `openai` provider with `CODEX_API_KEY`; I did not carry the older local `openai-codex` env-var hunk into this PR.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** OpenClaw gateway startup recovered from the stale file-backed SecretRef compatibility failure, and Telegram/Slack channel health remained live after the compatibility repair. The Telegram active-run ingress change is covered by the focused middleware test in this PR; media updates still stay on the normal serialized lane.
- **Real environment tested:** Local macOS OpenClaw setup on Hanson's Mini, OpenClaw runtime `2026.5.28`, gateway on `127.0.0.1:18789`, Telegram polling accounts `clawd2` through `clawd6` plus `default`, Slack `compbioatberkeley`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran live `openclaw gateway status --json`, `openclaw status --json`, and `openclaw gateway call channels.status --json --timeout 30000 --params '{}'` after repairing the stale SecretRefs and restarting the gateway.
- **Evidence after fix:** Live terminal output, redacted to omit secret values:
```json
{
  "gatewayStatus": {
    "runtime": { "status": "running", "state": "active", "pid": 60970 },
    "rpc": { "ok": true, "kind": "connect", "version": "2026.5.28" },
    "secretDiagnostics": null
  },
  "openclawStatus": {
    "runtimeVersion": "2026.5.28",
    "gatewayReachable": true,
    "gatewayError": null,
    "gatewayService": { "status": "running", "state": "active", "pid": 60970 },
    "nodeService": { "status": "running", "state": "active", "pid": 3652 },
    "taskAudit": { "total": 0, "warnings": 0, "errors": 0 },
    "secretDiagnostics": []
  },
  "channelsStatus": {
    "eventLoopDegraded": false,
    "telegram": [
      { "accountId": "clawd2", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "tokenStatus": "available", "mode": "polling" },
      { "accountId": "clawd3", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "tokenStatus": "available", "mode": "polling" },
      { "accountId": "clawd4", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "tokenStatus": "available", "mode": "polling" },
      { "accountId": "clawd5", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "tokenStatus": "available", "mode": "polling" },
      { "accountId": "clawd6", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "tokenStatus": "available", "mode": "polling" },
      { "accountId": "default", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "tokenStatus": "available", "mode": "polling" }
    ],
    "slack": [
      { "accountId": "compbioatberkeley", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "botTokenStatus": "available", "appTokenStatus": "available" }
    ]
  }
}
```
- **Observed result after fix:** Gateway RPC returned `ok: true`, `secretDiagnostics` was empty/null, task audit had `0` warnings and `0` errors, all six Telegram accounts were `running: true` and `connected: true` with no last error, and Slack `compbioatberkeley` was connected with both token statuses available.
- **What was not tested:** I did not exercise the PR branch against a live Telegram active-run from this checkout because the installed local runtime is `2026.5.28`; that path is covered by the focused Telegram middleware test in this PR.
target.author: hilo-hilo
